payload.author: claude
payload.body:
CORRECTION (verified against source): the earlier proposal's claim that 'the shipped web app.js has NO EventSource consumer — only iOS is realtime' is FALSE. The web UI IS realtime over the network: src/web/app.js:581 opens new EventSource('/events?workspace=...') with hello/change listeners, coalesced refresh, and active auto-repair on disconnect. The false negative came from grepping app.js without -a: it has long lines + Unicode glyphs so grep treats it as binary and silently matches nothing. FUTURE AGENTS: grep app.js with -a.

What IS true and still actionable: the SSE stream emits 'event: change
data: ...' with NO id: line (only retry: 2000 at server.js:457), so there is no Last-Event-ID resume — captured in SCP-147. The web client mitigates by re-fetching full board state on the next change event (self-heals on LAN); a real resume matters at WAN scale where full-refresh-per-event is costly. The 'reads files' part is the SERVER (hub watches .scope/ via fs.watch + data_version, fans out over the in-process bus); the browser consumes purely over HTTP/SSE.
